data.title: J&J Exterminating Celebrates 65th Anniversary and Unveils Strategic Vision at Annual Team Meeting
data.description: In Lafayette, Louisiana, on May 29, 2026, J&J Exterminating, a reliable company specializing in pest and termite control, celebrated its 65th anniversary at the annual team meeting in Spring 2026. The gathering took place at Doubletree by Hilton Hotel Lafayette, where numerous team members convened to commemorate this milestone.

data.title: OCI Global Q1 2026 Trading Update
data.description: In Amsterdam on May 29, 2026, Hassan Badrawi, the CEO of OCI Global, announced that during the initial quarter, OCI achieved significant advancements in implementing its strategic goals. These achievements included finalizing the sale of OCI Ammonia Holding to AGROFERT and transferring Beaumont New Ammonia to another entity. The CEO expressed satisfaction with the progress made, indicating a positive step forward for the company.

data.title: Analysts caution on Ethereum's downside with $1.8K as crucial level to monitor for support amid heightened risks.
data.description: Experts caution that the potential for Ether to experience a decline is high, with traders keeping a close eye on the support level of $1,800 for signs of stability.

data.title: HelloNation Highlights Morrisville as a Finalist for the National Civic League's 2026 All-America City Award
data.description: A report released on May 29, 2026, in Morrisville, North Carolina, sheds light on the town's innovative approaches to enhancing civic engagement. The focus is on digital tools, civic education programs, and community-centered public safety efforts. As Morrisville experiences rapid growth, the town aims to boost civic participation, leveraging technology advancements. Discussions include strategies for maintaining community involvement amidst evolving technological landscapes. The initiative addresses the challenge of balancing growth with effective citizen engagement.

data.title: Shackelford, McKinley & Norton Adds to Dallas, Houston Offices
data.description: Dallas-based law firm Shackelford, McKinley & Norton recently expanded its corporate law department by welcoming new partners in Dallas and Houston. Frank C. Ehman III, a seasoned transactions attorney, has joined the Dallas office, while Nathan Vrazel, a skilled real estate litigator, has become a part of the Houston team. This move aims to strengthen the firm's capabilities in serving clients from various industries.
